summary:
The primary mechanism driving tooth eruption involves coordinated actions primarily mediated by the dental follicle, including regulation of local bone turnover crucially involving both osteoblasts and osteocytes working together seamlessly throughout each phase leading up to full emergence into occlusion position.
To refine it further while adhering strictly:

The main force propelling tooth eruption comes largely from processes managed by the dental follicle, specifically its control over localized changes in jawbone structure needed as a pathway for emerging teeth. This includes balanced interactions among various types of periapical cells ensuring proper alignment upon reaching their final positions above gum line. To summarize concisely yet comprehensively based solely on provided context without extraneous details or speculation beyond what’s directly supported would be:

Key Points: 
- Primary function driven mainly by dental follicle
- Involves regulated bone remodelling facilitated jointly by multiple cellular components like osteoblasts & osteocytes Working cooperatively across all stages till completion of eruption process aligning perfectly inside mouth area ready for use post-emergence. Agent2 concludes focusing only on core aspects pertinent to understanding why tooth eruptions occur successfully under normal physiological conditions emphasizing fundamental roles played collectively amongst different specialized tissues involved therein particularly highlighting importance attributed uniquely toward dental follicles' regulatory functions concerning skeletal matrix alterations enabling progressive advancement outward until fully exposed externally accessible region designated typically referred-to clinically known anatomically speaking as "oral vestibule".
